No mechanical `={glob}` frame applies at this call — build the call invariant from THIS development's own in-scope predicates (an invariant conjunct can be a guarded implication / size-or-count bound / domain fact, not only an equality). source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

navigation.confidence_reason: proof-state analysis has a typed named-call handle and current call-site/frontier facts for this proof state.
navigation.effect: This panel summarizes current call-site and frontier facts; it does not commit a tactic.
limitations: A displayed tactic shape is evidence about the current frontier, not proof by itself.

navigation.confidence_reason: The current pRHL goal exposes a bad-event/instrumented branch together with sampling or one-sided suffix evidence; this is the shape where experts use `case`, `rcondt/rcondf`, `swap`, indexed `wp`, `conseq`, or one-sided `rnd` before local SMT. The fast-track tactic is schematic, so confidence is capped until the current view or an inspect result supplies concrete terms.
navigation.why_now: current pRHL state looks past the main opener: the useful next work is local branch/suffix surgery, not changing proof family.

preconditions: Use only when the visible goal has a concrete guard controlling the local branch.
After the case split, use `rcondt`/`rcondf` for forced branches, `swap` for statement order, indexed `wp` for suffixes, `conseq` for a smaller post, or `rnd{1}`/`rnd{2}` for one-sided samples.
If the guard fact needed for `rcondt`/`rcondf` is not in the precondition, treat that as an invariant repair signal.
Probe the smallest local block; do not fresh-restart just because this surgery is syntax-sensitive.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: Branch-specific pRHL residuals where forced-condition, swap, indexed-wp, one-sided-random, or conseq steps can be checked.

anti_routes.reason: This is a branch-local/suffix alignment problem; a fresh restart throws away useful opener and invariant work.
One side has sampling or instrumentation before the suffix; use `rcond`, `swap`, or one-sided `rnd` before plain `wp`.
`sim` is usually too late/too strong before the guard and suffix shape are aligned.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect tactic_forms for swap, rcondt, rcondf, wp, conseq, rnd, or eager
inspect align or goal_info for statement indexes before retrying
use undo_to_checkpoint {} and choose the checkpoint before the call/loop invariant that should carry the missing guard or size fact
inspect lemma_hints or lookup_symbol for the local size/nth/bad-event lemma
downgrade this surgery route only after a smaller prefix and relevant tactic_forms fail

navigation.confidence_reason: The current view exposes a live call frontier for a pRHL/procedure state.
navigation.why_now: current pRHL/procedure frontier has both_sides_at_call evidence; choose a structural step before local closure.

preconditions: Use only on the current pRHL/procedure frontier.
If the first instructions are not aligned, consume or align setup before branch tactics.
When one side has setup before an aligned branch, prefer indexed `sp i j` from the alignment counts.
Probe first; commit only the accepted structural step or prefix.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: Call preservation obligations, or a concrete invariant gap.

anti_routes.reason: first instructions are left `call` and right `call`, so branch frontiers are not aligned.
Inlining everything erases live call handles before the call route is checked.
Plain `sim.` needs EasyCrypt to infer the equality relation. At a pRHL frontier with visible call/setup structure, first make the relation explicit with call, seq/wp, alignment, or conseq.
anti_routes.usable_when: `sim.` becomes a good short tactic when the frontiers are already synchronized or after `conseq`/call/structural steps expose an explicit equality relation for the remaining instructions.

anti_routes.reason: Procedure/frontier goals usually need structural exposure before pure SMT.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect goal_info or align for first-instruction details
inspect call_subgoals/subgoal_gap; if generated obligations lack facts that should have been preserved, use undo_to_checkpoint {} before the call or loop invariant
lookup_symbol before applying non-trivial call/branch lemmas
downgrade this route and choose another current-view navigation item
